item.title: Monday, April 13 (today) — Week of 2026-04-13
item.description:
• 🔥 Project Glasswing: Securing critical software for the AI era — Anthropic officially unveils Claude Mythos Preview and Project Glasswing. Mythos is Anthropic's most capable model to date (93.9% SWE-bench Verified vs Opus 4.6's 80.8%; 83.1% on cybersecurity benchmarks vs 66.6%) — but it will NOT be publicly available. Instead, Anthropic launched a 12-member consortium (AWS, Apple, Broadcom, Cisco, CrowdStrike, Google, JPMorgan Chase, Linux Foundation, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Palo Alto Networks + Anthropic) with $100M in usage credits + $4M in open-source security donations to use Mythos purely for finding and patching vulnerabilities in critical infrastructure.
• 🛠 Claude Code v2.1.101: Team onboarding, OS CA cert trust, focus mode improvements — Latest CC release (April 11). Adds `/team-onboarding` command that generates a teammate ramp-up guide from your local CC usage history. OS CA certificate store is now trusted by default, so enterprise TLS proxies work without extra configuration. Brief mode now retries once when Claude responds with plain text instead of structured output. Focus mode writes more self-contained summaries.
• 🟡 MiniMax M2.7 open-weight model generating major community buzz — MiniMax M2.7 is dominating developer YouTube today as a high-performing open-source coding model for agents. Available with a free API key. Community note: it is not fully open-source — weights are available but training code/data are withheld. Being tested as a backend for "OpenClaw" and self-hosted CC-adjacent toolchains. Scores competitively on coding benchmarks against Qwen 3 and Llama 4.
• 🎥 Building an AI Dark Factory: A Codebase That Writes Its Own Code, Live — **Cole Medin** — Live session demonstrating a fully automated pipeline where Claude Code agents continuously write, test, and improve their own code with no human intervention. A concrete walk-through of the "AI factory" / self-improving codebase pattern that many teams are exploring.
• 🎥 Multica: This OPEN TOOL CONVERTS Claude, OpenCode into TEAMMATES! — **AICodeKing** (10 min) — Walkthrough of Multica, an open-source tool that wraps Claude Code and OpenCode into a collaborative multi-agent workflow. Useful for teams wanting agent-to-agent handoffs without writing custom orchestration code.

item.title: Sunday, April 12 — Week of 2026-04-06
item.description:
• 📰 Fail Safe: Why Anthropic won't release its new AI model — First major press wave on Claude Mythos: Anthropic is sitting on a frontier model so capable at finding and exploiting vulnerabilities that it decided against any public release. An unusually candid admission from the company. Full official Glasswing announcement drops April 13.
• 📰 At HumanX, everyone was talking about Claude — TechCrunch reports Claude dominated discussion at the HumanX AI conference in San Francisco — Claude ads on buildings, most enterprise conversations anchored around Anthropic. A signal of how rapidly mindshare has shifted since Claude Opus 4.6.
• 🟢 OpenAI releases gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b open-weight reasoning models — Two open-weight models for self-hosted or fine-tuned deployments: gpt-oss-120b for high-throughput production and gpt-oss-20b as a compact alternative. Both are reasoning-class models, available through major hosting providers. A new $100/month Pro plan also launched with unlimited GPT-5.4 and 10× Codex usage vs Plus.
• 🔵 Google launches Gemma 4, Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview, and Veo 3.1 Lite — Gemma 4 (26B MoE and 31B dense instruction-tuned variants) drops on AI Studio and the Gemini API under permissive licensing. Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite Preview is the first Flash-Lite model in the Gemini 3 series — designed for cost-efficient inference at scale. Veo 3.1 Lite arrives on Vertex AI as the most cost-efficient Veo video-gen model.
• 🎥 Forget Claude CoWork.. This NEW Autonomous Multi-Agent System is Better! (INSANE USE CASES) — **Rob The AI Guy** (11 min) — Compares an emerging open-source multi-agent framework against Claude Cowork, arguing it handles parallel subtasks with less scaffolding overhead. Worth watching to understand where the community is pushing agentic tooling.

item.title: Monday, March 30 — Week of 2026-03-30
item.description: • 🛠 Claude Code v2.1.88 — Major stability release with dozens of bug fixes and quality-of-life improvements.

item.title: Tuesday, March 31 — Week of 2026-03-30
item.description: • 💜 Australian Government and Anthropic Sign MOU for AI Safety and Research — Anthropic signed a Memorandum of Understanding with the Australian government covering AI safety cooperation and research collaboration.

item.title: Wednesday, April 1 — Week of 2026-03-30
item.description:
• 🛠 Claude Code v2.1.89 — Headless/automation improvements and April Fools fun.
• 🛠 Claude Code v2.1.90 — `/powerup` interactive lessons and critical bug fixes.

item.title: Thursday, April 2 — Week of 2026-03-30
item.description: • 🛠 Claude Code v2.1.91 — MCP result size control, plugin executables, and transcript reliability fixes.

item.title: Friday, April 4 — Week of 2026-03-30
item.description: • 🛠 Claude Code v2.1.92 — Remote settings enforcement, interactive Bedrock setup, per-model cost breakdown, and Linux sandboxing.

item.title: Monday, March 23 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description: • 🛠 Computer Use in Cowork and Claude Code — Claude can now control your Mac desktop: open apps, navigate browsers, fill spreadsheets, run dev tools -- all via keyboard and mouse, no setup required. When a direct connector isn't available, Claude falls back to pointing and clicking on your screen.

item.title: Tuesday, March 24 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description:
• 🛠 Auto mode for Claude Code — A new permissions mode where a background Sonnet 4.6 classifier reviews each tool call before execution, blocking actions that exceed scope (mass deletions, data exfiltration, malicious code). Middle ground between manual approvals and `--dangerously-skip-permissions`.
• 📝 Simon Willison: Auto mode for Claude Code — Simon expresses skepticism about AI-based permission classifiers being non-deterministic. Notes the allow list permits `pip install -r requirements.txt` which wouldn't protect against supply chain attacks. Advocates deterministic sandboxes over AI-based protections.
• 📝 Zvi Mowshowitz: Claude Code, Cowork and Codex #6 — Deep analysis of auto mode, computer use, and Channels launches. Emphasizes mundane utility (researchers building tools, families creating apps) while warning against running agents on unsandboxed machines with sensitive data.

item.title: Wednesday, March 25 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description:
• 🛠 CC 2.1.83: Transcript search, managed-settings.d/, and CwdChanged hooks — Press `/` in transcript mode to search conversations, navigate with `n`/`N`. New `managed-settings.d/` drop-in directory lets teams deploy policy fragments that merge alphabetically. `CwdChanged` and `FileChanged` hook events enable reactive environment management.
• 💜 Claude Code Channels: control CC from Telegram and Discord — Channels let you push events into a running Claude Code session via messaging platforms. Install a channel plugin, launch with `--channels`, and message Claude from your phone to trigger code tasks asynchronously.
• 🔧 Cursor ships self-hosted cloud agents — Cursor now supports self-hosted cloud agents that keep code and tool execution entirely in your own network. Your codebase, build outputs, and secrets stay on internal machines while the agent handles tool calls locally.
• 📝 Every Claude Code Update From March 2026, Explained — Builder.io comprehensive roundup of all March Claude Code changes including auto mode, channels, computer use, and every point release.

item.title: Thursday, March 26 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description:
• 🛠 CC 2.1.84: PowerShell tool, TaskCreated hook, and idle-return prompt — PowerShell tool for Windows arrives as opt-in preview. New `TaskCreated` hook fires when tasks are created. After 75+ minutes idle, Claude now nudges you toward `/clear` to avoid stale context.
• 🛠 CC 2.1.85: Conditional hooks, MCP OAuth RFC 9728, and scroll performance — Hooks now support conditional `if` field using permission rule syntax (e.g., `Bash(git *)`). MCP OAuth follows RFC 9728 Protected Resource Metadata discovery. Scroll performance overhauled -- replaced WASM yoga-layout with pure TypeScript.
• 🟢 OpenAI retires legacy deep research mode — The legacy deep research mode removed on March 26; the current deep research experience remains unchanged. Also: GPT-5.1 models fully retired since March 11, with conversations auto-migrated to GPT-5.3/5.4 equivalents.
• 🔵 Google Gemini Drop: chat history transfer and free Personal Intelligence — Import Memory to Gemini tool lets you migrate AI chat history from ChatGPT, Claude, and Copilot. Personal Intelligence (connecting Gmail, Photos, YouTube) now free for all US users. Gemini 3.1 Flash Live announced with improved voice and acoustic nuance recognition.
• 🔥 Claude Mythos model details leak via Anthropic CMS misconfiguration — A configuration error exposed ~3,000 unpublished assets, revealing "Claude Mythos" -- a new model tier (internal codename "Capybara") larger and more capable than Opus. Anthropic confirms it's piloting with early customers and calls it "a step change" in capabilities.

item.title: Friday, March 27 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description:
• 🛠 CC 2.1.86: Session ID header, reduced token usage, and memory UX — `X-Claude-Code-Session-Id` header added for proxy session aggregation. `@`-mentioned files no longer JSON-escaped (reduces token overhead). Read tool uses compact line-number format and deduplicates unchanged re-reads. Memory filenames highlight on hover and open on click.
• 🔧 GitHub Copilot agent mode GA on JetBrains IDEs — Agent mode graduates from beta to GA on all major JetBrains IDEs, bringing parity with VS Code. Includes custom agents, sub-agents, plan agent, semantic code search, and the GitHub issue-to-PR workflow.
• 📰 Anthropic confirms Claude Mythos model after data leak — Following the CMS leak, Anthropic officially confirms Claude Mythos is in testing with early access customers. The model is described as the most capable they've built, with a gradual rollout planned starting through the Claude API.
• 📰 Claude API outage: elevated errors on Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 — Networking performance degradation disrupted internal communication across the serving stack, causing elevated error rates for Opus 4.6 and Sonnet 4.6 users. Part of a broader Q1 pattern: 125 incidents in 90 days, median duration 1h13m, with Claude API at 99.04% uptime vs the previous >99.9% standard.
• 📝 Claude Code March 2026 Full Capability Interpretation — Comprehensive guide covering /loop scheduled tasks, Computer Use, Remote Control, and 12 core new features with practical examples.

item.title: Saturday, March 28 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description:
• 🛠 Claude March 2026 2x usage promotion ends today — The March 13-28 promotion that doubled usage limits during off-peak hours (outside 8AM-2PM ET weekdays, all day weekends) expires today. Applied to Free, Pro, Max, and Team plans across all Claude apps.
• 🔧 JetBrains Central: open platform for agentic software development — JetBrains announced Central, a control and execution plane for managing AI coding agents across teams. Connects developer tools, agents, and infrastructure with governance (policy enforcement, audit, cost attribution), cloud agent runtimes, and shared semantic context across repos.
• 🔧 Cursor ships self-hosted cloud agents and Automations — Cursor Automations lets you build always-on agents running on schedules or triggered by Slack, Linear, GitHub, PagerDuty, and webhooks. Self-hosted cloud agents keep code and tool execution entirely on your network. Combined with JetBrains IDE support (shipped March 4), Cursor is expanding beyond VS Code.
• 📝 Anthropic's madcap March: 14+ launches, 5 outages, and an accidental Claude Mythos leak — The New Stack's comprehensive March roundup: MCP crossed 97M monthly SDK downloads (up from ~2M at Nov 2025 launch), Claude Code usage grew 300% since Claude 4 models, run-rate revenue up 5.5x. 14+ product launches alongside 5 service outages.
• 📝 Claude Team is Shipping Like Crazy: 74 Releases in 52 Days — Product Compass breakdown of Anthropic's release velocity: 28 Claude Code releases, 15 desktop/Cowork, 18 API/infrastructure, 13 models/platform in under two months.

item.title: Sunday, March 29 — Week of 2026-03-23
item.description:
• 🛠 CC 2.1.87: Fix Cowork Dispatch message delivery — Critical hotfix for messages sent in Cowork Dispatch sessions not getting delivered. Users could send messages that were received and processed, but replies never appeared in the conversation.
• 🟢 TechCrunch: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora — Deep dive into why OpenAI killed its video generation product: user count collapsed from 1M peak to under 500K while burning ~$1M/day in compute. CEO Altman redirected resources to compete with Claude Code, which was "eating OpenAI's lunch" in the enterprise developer market.
• 📰 Claude API outage: ~4 hours of elevated errors — Claude experienced another significant service disruption on March 29, lasting approximately 3 hours 54 minutes starting around 8:13 PM ET. This marks the 6th major outage in March as part of the Q1 reliability decline (99.04% uptime vs previous >99.9%).
• 📰 Euronews: What is Anthropic's Mythos? — Continuing coverage of the Claude Mythos leak. Anthropic confirms the rollout timeline is "determined by safety evaluation outcomes" rather than a commercial schedule. Polymarket gives 45% odds of public release by June 30.
• 📝 Simon Willison: Python Vulnerability Lookup — Simon built an HTML tool with Claude Code that checks pyproject.toml or requirements.txt files against the OSV.dev vulnerability database. Paste your dependencies, see severity levels, affected versions, and disclosure links instantly.
